name: Current Affairs in History (3): India's 2nd Astronaut & the Mughal 500th Anniversary

sourceRefs.label: Wikipedia/oneindia.com - Shubhanshu Shukla's June 2025 spaceflight (Axiom Mission-4); Mughal Empire's 500th anniversary (1526-2026)

researchNotes: Third History current-affairs topic (3rd lap). The Shubhanshu Shukla fact is unusually valuable: it's a genuine historic FIRST/SECOND (India's 2nd-ever astronaut, 41 years after the first), cleanly dated, and naturally bridges History and Science — exactly the kind of durable 'current affairs become permanent history' fact this rotation track is meant to capture.

reading.intro: Covers a genuine historic milestone — India's second-ever astronaut — and a major historical commemoration: the Mughal Empire's 500th founding anniversary.

qa.question: Who became the SECOND Indian to travel to space, when, and how?
qa.answer: Wing Commander Shubhanshu Shukla, on 25 June 2025 — flying to the International Space Station (ISS) aboard SpaceX's Axiom Mission-4.

qa.question: Who was the FIRST Indian to travel to space, and when, for context?
qa.answer: Rakesh Sharma, in 1984, aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11 — meaning Shubhanshu Shukla's 2025 flight came 41 years after India's first astronaut.

qa.question: What major historical anniversary falls in 2026, and what does it mark?
qa.answer: The 500th anniversary of the founding of the Mughal Empire — founded on 21 April 1526 (following Babur's victory at the First Battle of Panipat). The empire's architectural legacy includes the Taj Mahal, Agra Fort, and Red Fort.

qa.question: What was 'Sewa Parv 2025', organised by the Ministry of Culture?
qa.answer: A cultural initiative held 17 September - 2 October 2025, featuring art workshops at 75 locations across India.
